kill_fact: No kill-fact found: every load-bearing figure independently verifies against the primary FY2026 results (Form 6-K / official press release dated 2026-05-12). The SoP is NOT a stale anchor — sop_fair_value=$150 is built on the freshest March-quarter/FY2026 print at the current ~$105 tape. Cloud rev RMB158,132M +34%, Cloud Q4 external +40% / EBITA +57%, AIDC loss narrowed to -RMB2,051M from -RMB15,137M, consolidated adj. EBITA -56% (RMB76,416M), FCF -RMB46,609M outflow (vs +RMB73,870M), capex RMB126,063M, cash+treasury RMB520,824M (US$75.5B), net cash ~RMB260.8B (~$38B), dividend US$1.05/ADS — ALL CORROBORATED. The thesis is genuinely cheap-on-SoP, but the doc itself honestly caps the edge: bear SoP (~$90-100) already equals the tape and a 15% China/VIE/governance haircut is embedded in the $150 base, so the structural-discount disqualifier (VIE shell, HFCAA/ADR-delisting tail, un-monetizable timing on Ant, capex-driven FCF hole) is acknowledged not hidden. Two corrections required: (a) the China-commerce segment the doc repeatedly calls 'TTG / Taobao and Tmall Group' was RESTRUCTURED in FY2026 and is now reported as 'Alibaba China E-commerce Group' (TTG + Ele.me + Fliggy) — the -44% EBITA (RMB107,509M vs RMB193,223M) is correct but for the COMBINED segment, so it is NOT a clean read of the core marketplace's standalone margin; (b) the '$19.1B remaining buyback through Mar-2027' is a Sept-30-2025 figure, ~6-9 months stale vs the FY2026 year-end print. Neither breaks the thesis; both warrant a value_conviction cap.
corrections: Segment renamed/restructured FY2026: 'TTG / Taobao and Tmall Group' is now reported as 'Alibaba China E-commerce Group' (TTG + Ele.me/Fliggy combined). The -44% adjusted EBITA (RMB107,509M vs RMB193,223M FY25) is the COMBINED-segment figure, so the SoP's 'normalized TTG EBITA' assumes a cleaner standalone marketplace margin than the reported line isolates — instant-retail (Ele.me/quick-commerce) losses are now baked into the same segment number the bull treats as a reversible reinvestment choice.

The '$19.1B buyback remaining through March 2027' is the Sept-30-2025 authorization balance, not the FY2026 year-end figure — directionally valid (program runs to Mar-2027) but ~6-9 months stale; remaining capacity is lower after continued FY2026 repurchases.

Net cash ~$38B verifies (cash+ST+treasury RMB520,824M less interest-bearing debt RMB259,996M = RMB260,828M ~ $37.8B), but the gross 'RMB520.8B / ~$72-75B liquidity' the bull cites is a GROSS figure including the debt that funds the AI build, not a net cushion — and capex is set to EXCEED the RMB380B plan, so the FCF outflow deepens before any asserted payback shows in cash.
evidence: Alibaba Group, 'March Quarter 2026 and Fiscal Year 2026 Results' (official press release / Form 6-K, 2026-05-12): Cloud Intelligence Group FY26 revenue RMB158,132M (+34%), adj. EBITA RMB14,265M (+35%); Q4 cloud external +40%, Q4 cloud adj. EBITA RMB3,796M (+57%).

Same release: Alibaba China E-commerce Group (formerly TTG) FY26 adj. EBITA RMB107,509M, -44% vs RMB193,223M FY25; AIDC adj. EBITA loss -RMB2,051M vs -RMB15,137M; consolidated adj. EBITA -56% (RMB76,416M); net income RMB102,127M vs RMB125,976M (-18.9%).

Same release: FY26 free cash flow OUTFLOW RMB46,609M (vs +RMB73,870M FY25); capital expenditures RMB126,063M; cash + short-term + treasury investments RMB520,824M (US$75,504M) at 2026-03-31; annual dividend US$1.05 per ADS; diluted EPS/ADS RMB44.00.
Alibaba Share Repurchase Update as of 2025-09-30 (Form 6-K): US$19.1B remaining under the repurchase authorization effective through March 2027 (figure predates the FY2026 year-end and is now stale).
